title: CI gate: GitHub Actions pytest on every PR + branch protection on main
description: Add an independent, agent-proof correctness gate that lives OUTSIDE the harness's trust boundary. Today the only test signal is the in-loop test stage's self-report plus the dual exit gate - all produced by the same stage agents that wrote the code, so a subtly-wrong agent can write a trivially-passing test, skip a failing one, or self-report green. Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `uv run pytest -q` on every pull request targeting main, so the same suite runs where no stage agent can edit, skip, or misreport it. This is the precondition that makes any future auto-merge defensible (S-010 / improvements C3 is the in-harness mirror; this is the out-of-harness one). NOTE: the file goes in .github/workflows/ci.yml - this is GitHub's CI directory and is distinct from the repo-root workflows/ directory that holds the harness orchestrators; do not put CI yaml in workflows/. Branch protection itself (requiring the check before merge to main) is a one-time human repo-admin action - agents cannot change repo settings or push to main - so the agent-runnable scope is the workflow file plus README docs; enabling branch protection is a documented manual human step, explicitly out of agent scope and never a blocker to report.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull_request targeting main, sets up Python 3.11 and uv, runs `uv sync` then `uv run pytest -q`, and fails the check on any test failure; it uses no secrets and no network beyond dependency install (the suite is offline per README)
The CI yaml lives in .github/workflows/ (GitHub's directory), never in the repo-root workflows/ directory that holds harness orchestrators

README documents the one-time manual branch-protection step (require the CI check to pass before merging to main) with exact gh api or UI instructions, and states this is the agent-proof gate that makes auto-merge defensible

The ticket's automatable scope is limited to the workflow file and README docs; branch-protection enablement is labeled a manual human/admin action and is explicitly out of agent scope, so a headless run neither attempts it nor reports blocked on it
When a PR is opened for the work branch, the CI workflow runs and passes (the suite is green under Actions, verified at the merge gate, not in-stage)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
